message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: I'm implementing multi-file support for click.edit(); I need to own _termui_impl.py and termui.py. What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: My task: add process_priority param to click.edit() and Editor class for CPU priority control of editor subprocess. These are the only 2 files my task touches. What files does your feature need? If you also need these two files, let's coordinate non-overlapping regions (e.g. Editor.__init__ vs Editor.edit_file body) rather than both files wholesale.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: We both genuinely need the same 2 files - true disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: since multi-file support is structurally invasive (changes filename param type/iteration logic) while mine is a small additive kwarg (process_priority) that only touches Editor.__init__ signature + the subprocess launch call in edit_file, let's go SEQUENTIAL not parallel. You implement multi-file support first and send DONE. I'll wait, then re-read the files fresh, layer process_priority on top of your changes (adding it as a trailing optional kwarg to edit()/Editor.__init__, and applying it around whatever subprocess launch call(s) exist after your edit), and send my own DONE. This avoids concurrent edits to the same lines. Sound good? If you've already started, tell me and I'll wait for your DONE before touching anything.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Genuine overlap confirmed, both need same 2 files. Proposing region split instead: (1) termui.py edit(): I add 'filename: str | Iterable[str] | None' + overloads; you append 'process_priority' as a new trailing kwarg + pass-through to Editor(...) -- keep your diff hunk separate from mine (different param, different line). (2) _termui_impl.py Editor.__init__: you own entirely (add process_priority attr there), I won't touch __init__. (3) _termui_impl.py edit_file->edit_files body: I own the rename + signature change + building/joining the multi-file command string (the 'c = subprocess.Popen(...)' line construction). You apply priority AFTER that Popen call returns (e.g. right after 'c = subprocess.Popen(...)', before 'exit_code = c.wait()', using c.pid) rather than editing the Popen call line itself -- that keeps our hunks non-overlapping. My method will be named edit_files(self, filenames) and the Popen result var stays named 'c'. Does this work for you?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed - going sequential. I have not started editing yet. I'll implement multi-file support now in src/click/_termui_impl.py and src/click/termui.py, then send DONE. You wait until you see my DONE, then re-read both files fresh and layer process_priority on top. Starting now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/termui.py (append process_priority trailing kwarg to edit() + pass to Editor()), src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.__init__ entirely, plus priority-apply logic inserted right after the subprocess.Popen(...) call returns as var 'c', before c.wait(), using c.pid — not touching the Popen line itself or the method name/signature)
your_files: src/click/termui.py (filename param type + overloads), src/click/_termui_impl.py (edit_file->edit_files rename, signature, Popen command construction)
note: Agreed. I'll re-read both files right before I start editing to pick up your latest state, and will only touch my agreed regions. Will send DONE when finished. Go ahead whenever.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Multi-file edit support complete and committed to patch.txt. Summary: Editor.edit_file(self, filename: str) renamed to Editor.edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py -- builds 'exec_filenames' by joining quoted filenames, then 'c = subprocess.Popen(f"{editor} {exec_filenames}", env=environ, shell=True)' (var still named 'c', right before 'exit_code = c.wait()' -- your priority hook goes there). Editor.__init__ untouched by me. In termui.py, edit() now takes filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None, with two @t.overload variants above the impl; body normalizes to a tuple and calls ed.edit_files(filenames). Safe for you to re-read both files now and layer process_priority on top.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/termui.py (edit() signature trailing process_priority kwarg + docstring + Editor(...) call kwarg), src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.__init__ + new _set_process_priority helper + one inserted line after 'c = subprocess.Popen(...)' in edit_file, before c.wait())
your_files: src/click/termui.py (filename type/overloads + multi-file body logic), src/click/_termui_impl.py (edit_file->edit_files rename/signature/Popen construction)
note: Heads up: I think our last two messages crossed in transit. You sent an ACCEPT proposing strict sequential (you go first, I wait for your DONE) right after your own REGION-SPLIT REVISE, which I had already ACCEPTed. I've ALREADY implemented my part under the region-split plan: added process_priority to termui.py edit() signature/docstring/Editor(...) call, and in _termui_impl.py added Editor.__init__ param + a new _set_process_priority(pid, priority) helper function + a single inserted line '_set_process_priority(c.pid, self.process_priority)' right after the existing 'c = subprocess.Popen(...)' line in edit_file, before 'exit_code = c.wait()'. I did NOT touch edit_file's signature, the Popen line itself, or anything filename-related. Please proceed with your multi-file support on top of current file state (git diff will show my hunks) - rename edit_file to edit_files etc as you planned; my inserted priority line uses 'c' and 'c.pid' so just carry it along if you restructure that block. Let me know if this works or if you need me to adjust anything. Sending DONE for my part now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/termui.py, src/click/_termui_impl.py
note: My process_priority implementation is complete (see previous message for exact details). Ready for you to layer/rebase multi-file support on top. Let me know if you hit any conflicts.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/termui.py, src/click/_termui_impl.py
note: FYI: I don't see your edit_files changes in my working tree, and there's no patch.txt here - looks like we're each in separate isolated repo copies, not a shared filesystem. So no real clobbering risk between us; each of us just submits our own patch.txt independently for the bench evaluator. My process_priority implementation is finished and verified (tests pass) in my copy. Good coordinating with you - no further action needed on my end.
